entries.title: patch_work_stage fail-fast reorder test (W-D) exercises the atomic function in isolation, not handler-level reordering — stronger test would simulate full handler
entries.source: qc3 S-1 targeted re-review (V1.39 P0); qc-consolidated.md
entries.note: Atomic-function test still proves the fail-fast property. Integration test would be more thorough but adds dependency on the surrounding handler. Accept as-is; document in test docstring if helper makes it visible.

entries.title: Boot-resume partial index existence verified but planner usage not empirically confirmed via EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN
entries.source: qc3 S-2 targeted re-review (V1.39 P0); qc-consolidated.md
entries.note: SQLite's planner may or may not use the partial index depending on table statistics; index is still safe (additive, no write overhead on paths that don't match the WHERE clause). Empirical EXPLAIN test can be added in a follow-up.

entries.title: ACH timestamp ID format %Y%m%d%H%M%S%3f lacks entropy — concurrent writers that both observe driver_schedule_id=null could collide in same millisecond
entries.closure_note: V1.40 P4 T2: appended AtomicU32 monotonic counter suffix to ACH schedule ID. No new deps. Commit c47d2125.
entries.note: Local-first single-user assumption lowers probability to near-zero. Fix: append ULID suffix or per-creator monotonic counter to the timestamp ID format. Helper is single source of truth (W-A) so the fix is one location.

entries.title: creator run resume only clears auto_chain_interrupted; no synchronous nudge/tick/enqueue creates observable 'resume requested but chain idle' window
entries.closure_note: V1.40 P4 T2: added auto_chain_interrupted to PatchWorkRequest + supervisor.tick() trigger on resume. Commit c47d2125.
entries.note: Fix: after clearing the auto_chain_interrupted flag, call supervisor.tick() (or a small kick() helper) so the resumed Work progresses in the same request rather than waiting for the next cycle. Could also fold into auto_chain::enqueue_auto_chain_schedule.

entries.title: tick_inner loads ALL creator_schedules rows regardless of status on every terminal event — auto-chain increases completion frequency and amplifies O(N) cost
entries.closure_note: V1.40 P4 T2: scoped tick_inner SELECT to status IN ('pending','running','paused'). Both query sites converted to runtime query_as with SAFETY comments. Commit c47d2125.
entries.note: Fix: scope the SELECT in tick_inner to pending|running statuses only. Auto-chain does not create new schedules with these statuses (it creates 'pending' or 'running' at enqueue, then transitions), so scope change is safe.
